message:
type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Adding process_priority param to click.edit()/Editor class for CPU priority control

type: CLAIM
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Implementing multi-file support for click.edit() - editing filename param type and Editor.edit_file/edit_files

type: QUESTION
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: I'm changing Editor.edit_file(filename) to Editor.edit_files(filenames) to support multi-file editing in click.edit(); will also touch edit() signature and subprocess call building the command line
blocked_on: need to know if your process_priority change touches edit_file's subprocess.Popen call signature so we don't clobber each other

type: ANSWER
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Yes, I modified edit_file's Popen call: added popen_kwargs (creationflags on Windows) passed into subprocess.Popen, and call self._apply_unix_priority(c.pid) right after Popen returns (before c.wait()). Also added process_priority param to Editor.__init__ and to click.edit() signature/docstring, plus two new Editor methods _get_windows_creationflags and _apply_unix_priority. If you rename edit_file to edit_files, please keep the popen_kwargs dict and the priority-apply call adjacent to each Popen invocation so priority still applies per-file.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Done with process_priority feature and wrote patch.txt; my edit_file/edit() changes are additive (new kwarg + priority hooks around existing Popen call), should merge cleanly with your edit_files rename if you keep those hooks in place.

type: STATUS
files: src/click/_termui_impl.py,src/click/termui.py
summary: Renamed edit_file(filename) to edit_files(filenames: Iterable[str]); still ONE subprocess.Popen call per invocation (all filenames joined as separate quoted args on the same command line, not one Popen per file) - your popen_kwargs dict and _apply_unix_priority(c.pid) call can go right after that single Popen call unchanged. edit() in termui.py now normalizes filename (str or Iterable[str]) into a list and calls ed.edit_files(filenames=...). Will submit my patch.txt now; happy to adjust if your merge needs a different shape.
